issues.description: Using deprecated n1-standard-4 machine type. The N1 series is legacy and offers lower performance-per-dollar compared to N2/E2 series.
issues.suggestion: Migrate to n2-standard-4 for 20% better performance at similar cost

issues.description: No deletion_protection enabled on production database. Accidental terraform destroy could delete the database.
issues.suggestion: Add deletion_protection = true to prevent accidental deletion

issues.description: No labels/tags defined for resource organization and cost tracking
issues.suggestion: Add labels for environment, team, cost-center

issues.description: Using db-n1-standard-2 tier which is legacy
issues.suggestion: Consider db-custom or newer tier options

issues.description: Firewall rule allows inbound traffic from 0.0.0.0/0 on ports 80 and 443. This exposes services to the entire internet without IP filtering.
issues.suggestion: Restrict source_ranges to known IP ranges or use Cloud Armor for web application firewall

issues.description: Internal firewall allows all ports (0-65535) on TCP and UDP within 10.0.0.0/8
issues.suggestion: Restrict to only required ports for inter-service communication

issues.description: No logging enabled on firewall rules
issues.suggestion: Enable firewall logging for security monitoring

issues.description: Bucket has uniform_bucket_level_access disabled and CORS allows all origins. This creates a public bucket accessible from anywhere.
issues.suggestion: Enable uniform_bucket_level_access and restrict CORS origins

issues.description: No CMEK encryption configured. Data is encrypted with Google-managed keys only.
issues.suggestion: Configure Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) using Cloud KMS

issues.description: No lifecycle rules configured. Logs will accumulate indefinitely, increasing storage costs.
issues.suggestion: Add lifecycle rule to transition to Nearline after 30 days and delete after 365 days

issues.description: No CMEK encryption on logs bucket containing potentially sensitive application data
issues.suggestion: Apply CMEK encryption using the existing db_key or a dedicated logs encryption key

issues.description: Service account bound to roles/editor — this grants broad read/write access to almost all GCP resources. Violates principle of least privilege.
issues.suggestion: Replace with specific roles: roles/compute.instanceAdmin, roles/cloudsql.client, roles/storage.objectViewer

issues.description: roles/storage.admin grants full control over ALL storage buckets in the project
issues.suggestion: Use roles/storage.objectAdmin on specific buckets instead of project-level binding
Add detailed description of the service account's purpose and permissions scope

issues.description: Using Cloud Functions Gen1 (google_cloudfunctions_function). Gen2 offers better performance, longer timeouts, and event-driven triggers.
issues.suggestion: Migrate to google_cloudfunctions2_function for Gen2 features

issues.description: Using Python 3.9 runtime which is approaching end of support
issues.suggestion: Upgrade to python311 or python312 runtime

issues.description: SSH key has a default value hardcoded in the variable definition
issues.suggestion: Remove default value and require it to be provided at apply time

recommendations: CRITICAL: Replace roles/editor with task-specific IAM roles immediately
Upgrade all n1 machine types to n2 or e2 equivalents for better performance
Enable CMEK encryption on all Cloud Storage buckets using existing KMS key ring
Add deletion_protection = true to the PostgreSQL instance
Restrict media_assets bucket: enable uniform_bucket_level_access and limit CORS
Add lifecycle rules to the logs bucket (30-day transition, 365-day deletion)
Migrate Cloud Functions from Gen1 to Gen2 for improved performance
Restrict firewall rules: use Cloud Armor WAF instead of open 0.0.0.0/0 rules
Enable firewall logging on all rules for security monitoring
